dji-neo-molse8lq.oneLiner: 135g palm-launch selfie drone for social creators who want zero-fuss AI tracking.
keySpecs: 135g — no pilot certificate required in Switzerland
1/2-inch CMOS, 12MP, 4K/30fps at 75Mbps (JPEG only, no RAW)
~18 min flight time; 22GB internal storage only (no microSD slot)
No obstacle avoidance; downward visual positioning only
Launches from palm; flies controller-free via app or voice
pros: Lowest barrier to entry of any DJI drone — controller-free, palm launch
Built-in propeller guards make it safe around people
AI subject tracking and QuickShots produce social-ready clips with no piloting skill
cons: No obstacle avoidance — will fly into trees and walls
No microSD slot; 22GB internal fills fast at 4K; no RAW photos
Weak in wind above ~18 mph; not an outdoor adventure drone
dji-neo-molse8lq.verdict: Best pick for first-time pilots who want zero paperwork and maximum portability.

dji-mini-4k-molsexo9.oneLiner: 246g folding drone with 4K, 3-axis gimbal and 31 min flight for budget beginners.
keySpecs: 246g — operator registration required in Switzerland (CHF 10, no pilot exam)
1/2.3-inch CMOS, 12MP, f/2.8, 4K/30fps at 100Mbps
3-axis mechanical gimbal — genuinely smooth stabilised footage
31 min rated flight (~20–25 min real-world); Level 5 wind resistance (38 km/h)
10 km O3 transmission; downward sensors only — no obstacle avoidance
pros: 3-axis mechanical gimbal delivers smooth footage no fixed-camera competitor can match
Level 5 wind resistance handles moderate outdoor conditions reliably
QuickShots and auto Return-to-Home make it beginner-safe out of the box
cons: No obstacle avoidance — only downward sensors for landing assist
1/2.3-inch sensor struggles in low light and blows out high-contrast scenes
Requires smartphone as screen with RC-N1 remote
dji-mini-4k-molsexo9.verdict: Best first outdoor drone under CHF 300; skip if you need obstacle avoidance.

dji-mini-4-pro-molsf9kz.oneLiner: Sub-250g prosumer drone with omnidirectional avoidance for travel creators.
keySpecs: 249g — no C1 designation, no mandatory insurance in CH
1/1.3-inch CMOS, 48MP, f/1.7 aperture, Dual Native ISO
4K/100fps, 4K/60fps HDR, 10-bit D-Log M/HLG
34 min flight time (45 min with Plus battery); ~30 min real-world
Omnidirectional obstacle avoidance — 6 fisheye sensors + 3D infrared
DJI O4 transmission, 20 km range, true vertical shooting mode
pros: Best-in-class omnidirectional obstacle avoidance in the sub-250g category
4K/100fps slow-motion and 10-bit D-Log M in a 249g body
True vertical shooting mode (45° camera rotation) for social content
20 km O4 range — far exceeds practical needs
cons: Low-light noise visible at 100% crop — not a low-light specialist
No AirSense transponder — cannot detect nearby manned aircraft
Significantly more expensive than the Mini 4K for casual users
dji-mini-4-pro-molsf9kz.verdict: Best sub-250g all-rounder; skip only if you already own the Mini 3 Pro.

dji-lito-x1-monf41w7.oneLiner: Budget 249g beginner drone with LiDAR, 4K/100fps and 42GB internal storage.
keySpecs: 249g — no C1 designation, no mandatory insurance in CH
1/1.3-inch CMOS, 48MP, f/1.7 aperture
4K/60fps HDR, 4K/100fps slow-mo, 10-bit D-Log M (14 stops DR)
36 min rated flight time; omnidirectional vision + forward LiDAR (0.5–36m)
42GB internal storage built-in — no SD card needed
DJI O4, 15 km range; €419 drone-only (CHF 378 at dji.com/ch)
pros: Forward-facing LiDAR unique at this price — stronger low-light obstacle detection
42GB internal storage eliminates the need for an SD card
10-bit D-Log M and 4K/100fps at an entry-level price point
Rated #1 beginner drone by TechRadar at launch (Apr 2026)
cons: Image quality below Mini 5 Pro and Air 3S — not a professional tool
Digital zoom degrades noticeably — fixed focal length only practical option
LiDAR and obstacle avoidance disabled in Sport mode
dji-lito-x1-monf41w7.verdict: Best-value beginner drone in Europe; strong buy under €420 for Swiss buyers.
